# Golarion — Major Cities Reference

Structured reference for the AI GM's settlement generation, shopping, NPC placement, and geographic context.
Covers major Inner Sea and Tian Xia cities the AI GM needs to know.

---

## Absalom — City at the Center of the World

- **Nation:** Absalom & Starstone Isle
- **Population:** ~300,000 (largest city in the Inner Sea)
- **Government:** Grand Council led by Primarch
- **Notable Districts:**
  - **Ascendant Court** — Starstone Cathedral at center; temples of all major faiths
  - **Coins** — Merchant quarter, Grand Bazaar, money changers
  - **Foreign Quarter** — Embassies, immigrant communities, exotic goods
  - **Ivy District** — Wealthy residential, noble estates
  - **Petal District** — Entertainment, theaters, taverns
  - **Precipice Quarter** — Ruined district (earthquake damage), adventurer hangout, monster-infested
  - **Docks** — Harbor, warehouses, sailor taverns, smuggling
  - **Wise Quarter** — Arcanamirium (wizard school), Pathfinder Grand Lodge
- **Key NPCs/Institutions:**
  - Pathfinder Grand Lodge (adventurer hub)
  - Arcanamirium (premier wizard academy)
  - Starstone Cathedral (site of divine ascension tests)
  - Grand Bazaar (can find almost anything for sale)
- **Available Services:** Everything. Any item up to level 20 can theoretically be found here. Master-level crafters, the finest healers, legendary trainers.
- **Shop Level Cap:** 15+ (specialty shops may stock higher)
- **Campaign Relevance:** The default starting city for campaigns. Cosmopolitan, every faction represented, and the Precipice Quarter provides a dungeon right in the city. Gateway to anywhere by sea.

---

## Almas — Cradle of Liberty

- **Nation:** Andoran
- **Population:** ~76,000
- **Government:** People's Council (democratic)
- **Notable Districts:**
  - **Liberty District** — Government buildings, Council Hall
  - **Portside** — Harbor, trade warehouses
  - **Old Town** — Residential, historical buildings from Chelish era
- **Key NPCs/Institutions:**
  - People's Council Hall
  - Eagle Knight headquarters
  - Gray Corsair naval base
- **Available Services:** Good selection of martial and naval equipment. Standard magical services.
- **Shop Level Cap:** 10-12
- **Campaign Relevance:** Political intrigue hub. Democratic politics, anti-slavery operations launch from here, Eagle Knight missions. Good starting city for freedom-themed campaigns.

---

## Egorian — Cheliax's Throne of Shadows

- **Nation:** Cheliax
- **Population:** ~82,000
- **Government:** Imperial Court of House Thrune
- **Notable Districts:**
  - **Imperial District** — Palace, noble estates, devil-guarded gates
  - **Chain District** — Slave markets, labor camps
  - **Midnight District** — Asmodean temples, infernal summoning circles
  - **Trade Quarter** — Wealthy merchants, legal commerce
- **Key NPCs/Institutions:**
  - House Thrune imperial palace
  - Asmodean cathedral
  - Hellknight citadels nearby
- **Available Services:** Excellent for diabolist magic, infernal contracts, poison, and dark items. Standard martial goods.
- **Shop Level Cap:** 12-14 (illicit items higher)
- **Campaign Relevance:** The heart of evil empire intrigue. Visiting Egorian is dangerous for good-aligned characters. Excellent setting for infiltration, espionage, and moral compromise scenarios.

---

## Magnimar — City of Monuments

- **Nation:** Varisia (city-state)
- **Population:** ~16,000
- **Government:** Council of Ushers and Lord-Mayor
- **Notable Districts:**
  - **Summit** — Wealthy hilltop district, noble estates, government
  - **Shore** — Middle-class neighborhoods, shops, taverns
  - **Shadow** — Underside of the Irespan (ancient Thassilonian bridge), criminal underworld
  - **Dockway** — Harbor, trade, sailors
  - **Bazaar of Sails** — Open-air market for exotic goods
- **Key NPPs/Institutions:**
  - The Irespan (massive Thassilonian ruin spanning the harbor — ancient mystery)
  - Pathfinder Lodge
  - Golemworks (construct crafters)
  - Bazaar of Sails (can find unusual items)
- **Available Services:** Good general selection. Golemworks for construct-related services. Thassilonian artifacts occasionally surface.
- **Shop Level Cap:** 8-10
- **Campaign Relevance:** Rise of the Runelords starting city. Thassilonian mysteries, the Irespan, Sczarni criminal activity, and frontier adventuring into Varisia. Progressive city with a dark underbelly.

---

## Korvosa — Jewel of Varisia

- **Nation:** Varisia (city-state)
- **Population:** ~18,000
- **Government:** Monarchy (recently turbulent)
- **Notable Districts:**
  - **Castle District** — Castle Korvosa, noble estates
  - **Heights** — Wealthy residential, Acadamae (wizard school)
  - **Midland** — Middle class, government buildings, markets
  - **Old Korvosa** — Oldest district, declining, criminal activity
  - **North Point** — Merchants, temples
  - **East Shore** — Working class
- **Key NPCs/Institutions:**
  - Acadamae (devil-binding wizard school — Chelish traditions)
  - Sable Company (hippogriff-riding marines)
  - Korvosan Guard
  - Gray Maidens (controversial all-female royal guard)
- **Available Services:** Strong arcane services (Acadamae). Good martial equipment. Chelish luxuries.
- **Shop Level Cap:** 8-10
- **Campaign Relevance:** Curse of the Crimson Throne setting. More rigid and Chelish-influenced than Magnimar. The Acadamae provides wizard-school intrigue. Recent political upheaval makes it ripe for adventure.

---

## Oppara — The Gilded City

- **Nation:** Taldor
- **Population:** ~109,000
- **Government:** Grand Prince/Princess and Senate
- **Notable Districts:**
  - **Crown District** — Imperial palace, senate, noble estates
  - **Grandbridge** — Theaters, galleries, high culture
  - **Mercato** — Markets, middle-class commerce
  - **Westpark** — Gardens, noble residences
  - **Narrows** — Poor district, criminal activity, forgotten by nobility
- **Key NPCs/Institutions:**
  - Imperial Palace (seat of the declining empire)
  - Senate (Taldan Phalanx — scheming nobility)
  - Lion Blades (royal spies and assassins)
  - Grand Opera House
- **Available Services:** Luxurious goods, fine art, courtly equipment. Decent magical services.
- **Shop Level Cap:** 10-12
- **Campaign Relevance:** Decadent imperial capital. Political intrigue among noble houses, Lion Blade espionage, the contrast between obscene wealth and grinding poverty. The Narrows is whole district the nobility pretends doesn't exist.

---

## Katapesh (City) — Market of Marvels

- **Nation:** Katapesh
- **Population:** ~212,000
- **Government:** The Pactmasters (alien merchant lords)
- **Notable Districts:**
  - **The Daystalls** — Legal open-air market (anything not explicitly banned)
  - **The Nightstalls** — Infamous black market (anything at all, including slaves and forbidden magic)
  - **Dawn Gate** — Wealthy merchant estates
  - **Twilight Gate** — Working class, caravansaries
  - **Dog Town** — Gnoll quarter
- **Key NPCs/Institutions:**
  - The Pactmasters (mysterious alien rulers — veiled, unknown species)
  - Nightstalls (black market)
  - Pesh dealers (the drug trade is semi-legal)
- **Available Services:** If it exists, Katapesh sells it. No moral restrictions on commerce. Slave markets, banned items, stolen artifacts — all available at a price.
- **Shop Level Cap:** 14+ (if you can pay and find the right seller)
- **Campaign Relevance:** The ultimate marketplace. Need something illegal, exotic, or impossible? Try Katapesh. The moral compromise of shopping here is itself a campaign element. The Pactmasters' true nature is a mystery worth investigating.

---

## Sothis — City of the Pharaohs

- **Nation:** Osirion
- **Population:** ~111,000
- **Government:** Ruby Prince Khemet III
- **Notable Districts:**
  - **Palace District** — Royal palace, temples
  - **Black Dome** — Nethys temple (massive observatory/library)
  - **Sphinx District** — Sphinx-guarded ancient quarter with tombs beneath
  - **Souk** — Sprawling marketplace
- **Key NPCs/Institutions:**
  - Ruby Prince's court (grants tomb exploration licenses)
  - Black Dome (Nethys temple, magical research)
  - Tomb exploration guilds
- **Available Services:** Ancient magical items surface from tombs. Good alchemical services. Tomb exploration gear.
- **Shop Level Cap:** 10-12 (tomb finds can be much higher)
- **Campaign Relevance:** Indiana Jones meets ancient Egypt. The Ruby Prince licenses tomb exploration, creating a legal adventuring economy. Ancient curses, tomb guardians, and rival explorer factions provide constant adventure hooks.

---

## Caliphas — Gothic Capital

- **Nation:** Ustalav
- **Population:** ~60,000
- **Government:** County seat, nominally under Ustalavic crown
- **Notable Districts:**
  - **Castle District** — County seat, noble families
  - **Eskcourt** — Wealthy quarter, vampire-infiltrated salons
  - **Leeward** — Harbor, trade, sailors' quarter
  - **Monastery Hill** — Pharasmin cathedral, undead defense
- **Key NPCs/Institutions:**
  - Pharasma's Cathedral (undead hunting headquarters)
  - Vampire salons (open secret among nobility)
  - Esoteric Order of the Palatine Eye (secret lodge)
- **Available Services:** Anti-undead equipment, holy water, silver weapons always in stock. Gothic fashion, horror-themed supplies.
- **Shop Level Cap:** 8-10
- **Campaign Relevance:** Gothic horror capital. Vampires mingling with nobility at salons, Pharasmin undead hunters, Palatine Eye occultists, and Whispering Way infiltrators. Every dark alley has something lurking in it.

---

## Goka — Jewel of the East

- **Nation:** Free city (Tian Xia)
- **Population:** ~300,000+
- **Government:** Council of factions and guilds
- **Notable Districts:**
  - **The Floating Market** — Legendary waterborne marketplace on interconnected boats and platforms
  - **Embassy Row** — Foreign delegations, Inner Sea trading companies
  - **Old Quarter** — Ancient temples, spirit shrines, traditional architecture
  - **Deepmarket** — Underground/underwater market and dungeon complex beneath the city
  - **Harbor of Lights** — Main port, lantern-lit docks
- **Key NPCs/Institutions:**
  - Trade Council (competing merchant factions)
  - Deepmarket (underground commerce and danger)
  - Temples to every Tian Xia deity
  - Inner Sea embassies (Absalom, Cheliax, Andoran)
- **Available Services:** Anything from Tian Xia plus Inner Sea imports. Eastern and western magical traditions both available.
- **Shop Level Cap:** 15+ (comparable to Absalom)
- **Campaign Relevance:** The eastern Absalom. Gateway between the Inner Sea and Tian Xia. The Floating Market and Deepmarket are iconic settings. Every Tian Xia faction has agents here. Cross-cultural adventures are the norm.

---

## Kasai — Minkai's Restored Throne

- **Nation:** Minkai
- **Population:** ~120,000
- **Government:** Emperor/Empress (Higashiyama dynasty)
- **Notable Districts:**
  - **Imperial Palace** — Restored Higashiyama seat of power
  - **Samurai Quarter** — Warrior estates, dojos, weapon smiths
  - **Temple District** — Shizuru and Tsukiyo shrines, kami sanctuaries
  - **Merchant Ward** — Markets, crafters, geisha houses
  - **Shadow Quarter** — Ninja clan territory (officially doesn't exist)
- **Key NPCs/Institutions:**
  - The Imperial Court (recently restored, factional)
  - Great dojos (samurai training academies)
  - Ninja clans (operate from the shadows)
  - Kami shrines
- **Available Services:** Exquisite weapons (katana, wakizashi), samurai armor, Eastern magical traditions. Ninja tools available through... channels.
- **Shop Level Cap:** 10-12
- **Campaign Relevance:** Samurai court drama, ninja intrigue, post-Jade Regent rebuilding. The tension between traditional order and the chaos left by the oni occupation creates rich adventure hooks.

---

## Settlement Size Quick Reference

For the AI GM to determine available goods and services based on settlement type:

| Settlement Type | Population | Max Item Level | Typical Services |
|----------------|-----------|---------------|-----------------|
| Thorp | < 20 | 1 | Basic supplies only |
| Hamlet | 21–60 | 2 | General store, maybe a healer |
| Village | 61–400 | 3-4 | Inn, temple, basic smith |
| Small Town | 401–2,000 | 5-6 | Specialized shops, church, alchemist |
| Large Town | 2,001–5,000 | 7-8 | Multiple temples, magic items, trainers |
| Small City | 5,001–25,000 | 9-10 | Full services, specialty shops, academies |
| Large City | 25,001–100,000 | 11-13 | Extensive services, rare items occasionally |
| Metropolis | 100,001+ | 14+ | Nearly anything available with effort |

**Notes for the AI GM:**
- Frontier settlements stock survival gear and weapons but not luxury items
- Coastal cities have more exotic foreign goods
- Desert cities stock water, sun protection, and desert survival gear prominently
- Evil-aligned cities stock poisons, unholy items, and slaves
- Religious cities stock divine items matching their patron deity
- Prices at remote settlements may be 10-50% higher due to supply difficulty
